```python
import pytest

from your_module import rotate_list  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual module name


def test_happy_path():
    """Test basic right rotation with valid inputs."""
    assert rotate_list([1, 2, 3, 4], 2) == [3, 4, 1, 2]
    assert rotate_list([1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 3) == [3, 4, 5, 1, 2]


def test_empty_list():
    """Test rotation on an empty list."""
    assert rotate_list([], 0) == []
    assert rotate_list([], 5) == []
    assert rotate_list([], -3) == []


def test_k_zero():
    """Test rotation by 0 positions (no change)."""
    assert rotate_list([1, 2, 3], 0) == [1, 2, 3]
    assert rotate_list([1], 0) == [1]


def test_k_equals_length():
    """Test rotation by exactly the length of the list (no change)."""
    assert rotate_list([1, 2, 3], 3) == [1, 2, 3]
    assert rotate_list([1, 2, 3, 4], 4) == [1, 2, 3, 4]


def test_k_larger_than_length():
    """Test rotation with k larger than list length (uses modulo)."""
    assert rotate_list([1, 2, 3], 5) == [2, 3, 1]  # 5 % 3 = 2
    assert rotate_list([1, 2, 3, 4], 6) == [3, 4, 1, 2]  # 6 % 4 = 2


def test_negative_k():
    """Test rotation with negative k (Python modulo makes it positive equivalent)."""
    # -1 % 3 = 2, so right rotate by 2
    assert rotate_list([1, 2, 3], -1) == [2, 3, 1]
    # -4 % 3 = 2, same as above
    assert rotate_list([1, 2, 3], -4) == [2, 3, 1]
    # -3 % 3 = 0, no change
    assert rotate_list([1, 2, 3], -3) == [1, 2, 3]


def test_single_element_list():
    """Test rotation on a list with one element."""
    assert rotate_list([42], 0) == [42]
    assert rotate_list([42], 1) == [42]
    assert rotate_list([42], 10) == [42]
    assert rotate_list([42], -5) == [42]


def test_non_list_input():
    """Test error case: non-list input for lst."""
    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="can only concatenate list"):
        rotate_list("abc", 1)
    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="can only concatenate list"):
        rotate_list(123, 1)
    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="can only concatenate list"):
        rotate_list(None, 1)


def test_non_int_k():
    """Test error case: non-integer input for k."""
    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="unsupported operand type"):
        rotate_list([1, 2, 3], "1")
    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="unsupported operand type"):
        rotate_list([1, 2, 3], 1.5)  # Slicing with float index raises TypeError
    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="unsupported operand type"):
        rotate_list([1, 2, 3], None)


def test_none_inputs():
    """Test error cases: None for lst or k."""
    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="can only concatenate list"):
        rotate_list(None, 1)
    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="unsupported operand type"):
        rotate_list([1, 2, 3], None)
```
